title: Generation-2 evolution audit: G6 (ISO_FMT sub-second truncation), G7 (recontext abs-path logging), G8 (update --artifact drops repeats), P-003 residual closure review
description: claude.md 5A audit over ALL state.json evolution.next_audit_inputs. Parse events.jsonl + transcript.jsonl (now hook-fed with real traffic, see T-011 evidence in tasks/T-011.json). Deliver .harness/logs/audit_gen2.md with quoted evidence, failure taxonomy, and falsifiable ranked proposals split code-level vs NLAH-gated. PROPOSE ONLY - do not apply.

notes.note: Gen-2 audit: (1) reproduce G6 ISO_FMT sub-second TTL race arithmetically from harness_common; (2) quote G7 recontext.py abs-path lock lines + events sample; (3) demonstrate G8 --artifact single-value drop from argparse + static reason; (4) verify P-003 residual via T-011.json + real transcript session 15636cc2; (5) fresh trajectory sweep of events/transcript for NEW failure modes. Deliverable audit_gen2.md; split code-level vs NLAH-gated; rank + disjoint worker partition.

HANDOFF -> verifier: Gen-2 audit at .harness/logs/audit_gen2.md; every claim cites file:line or a read-only python3 -c repro (no bin/ file modified, no live task mutated). VERDICT-READY REPLAY: (G6/P-005) harness_common.py:30/:38/:147 -> import-only repro shows acquire@X.999 --ttl 1 => effective_life 0.001s; live deviation T-010.json:21,:26 (agents forced ttl3). (G8/P-007) blackboard.py:407 store-not-append -> argparse repro '--artifact A --artifact B'=>'B' (A dropped); real 2-call workaround events.jsonl:120,:122. (G7/P-008) recontext.py:47/:58/:84/:105 abs path -> events.jsonl:116/117/160/161 absolute vs lock.py:66/:98 relative events.jsonl:101/129. (N1/P-006 NEW) lock.py:50-60 & recontext.py:68-78 busy branch has no log_event; grep -in busy events.jsonl=0 yet T-010.json:26 shows a real busy exit1 -> contention unobservable. (P-003 CLOSED) T-011 done, verifier verdict 2026-07-04T06:20:55Z; transcript.jsonl 74 real session-15636cc2 lines vs 1 synthetic. ALL 4 proposals code-level, NO human gate (no claude.md/gemini.md edit). Suggested DISJOINT partition: WA=harness_common.py+T-010.json+README.md; WB=lock.py+recontext.py; WC=blackboard.py. Verifier: re-run the two python3 -c repros + grep the cited lines.

REJECTED: P-008/G7 evidence fails replay. Audit $2.3 & $5 claim grep -c '/Users/' events.jsonl = 4 (all recontext); ACTUAL = 9. All 9 predate the 06:25:21Z handoff (latest ev ts 06:20:14Z) so this is NOT log-drift - the '4' was wrong when written. Only 4/9 are recontext lock events (ev:116/117/160/161, recontext_evidence.md). Other 5: ev:199 notify_config_initialized (notify.py, abs path) + ev:226-229 lock.py lock_acquired/released on /Users/.../scratch/verify_T-011.txt (T-011, caller passed abs path). Those 4 lock.py events REFUTE P-008's diagnosis 'every other lock event is repo-relative' and its measurable 'all future lock-event path fields are relative' -- lock.py logs path=args.path verbatim, so caller-supplied abs paths persist regardless of the recontext fix. Required fix: correct count 4->9, and either narrow the diagnosis (recontext is one of >=3 abs-path emitters) or extend the fix to lock.py's caller path. REPLAYS CLEAN (6/7): P-005/G6 import-repro reproduces audit $3 exactly (acquire@X.999 --ttl1 -> effective_life 0.001s; --ttl3 -> 2.001s; harness_common.py:30/38/147 floor-vs-microsecond asymmetry confirmed; T-010.json:21,:26 = 2 'per G6' TTL-override handoff notes). P-006/N1: lock.py:50-60 & recontext.py:68-78 busy branch return 1/False with no hc.log_event; grep -c lock_busy=0. P-007/G8: argparse store keeps only B (A dropped), append preserves both; blackboard.py:407 default store confirmed. P-003: T-011 done, verified by harness-verifier != producer fable-5-coordinator @06:20:55Z; transcript 116 real 15636cc2 lines. Cross-claims: 2 stole_expired (ev:173/188), 1 claim_expired (ev:177), 0 non-Z ts, 0 out-of-order across 267 events (audit's 225/4 are stale snapshots but invariants hold). WA/WB/WC partition genuinely disjoint (harness_common.py+T-010.json+README->WA; lock.py+recontext.py->WB; blackboard.py->WC). Gate class correct: no claude.md/gemini.md edit. ONLY P-008's false grep count + refuted diagnosis blocks acceptance.

Re-claimed after REJECTED (P-008 only). Re-verifying /Users/ path counts independently, re-diagnosing G7 as log-boundary normalization gap (lock.py+recontext.py+notify.py), refreshing stale live counts. P-005/P-006/P-007 untouched.

HANDOFF -> verifier: revision (Rev-2): P-008 rediagnosed per REJECTED note; P-005/P-006/P-007 UNCHANGED (they passed replay verbatim). P-008 corrections, all independently re-verified: (1) grep -c '/Users/' events.jsonl = 9 (Rev-1 wrongly said 4 — I had counted only recontext hits, never the global set); ALL 9 predate the Rev-1 handoff (latest 06:20:14Z < 06:25:21Z) so it is a standing leak, not drift. (2) Breakdown: 4 recontext (ev116/117/160/161, hardcoded EVIDENCE_PATH recontext.py:47), 1 notify (ev199, notify.py:200 path=str(CONFIG_PATH)), 4 lock.py CALLER-supplied absolute (ev226-229, lock.py:66/:98 path=args.path verbatim). (3) Rediagnosis: missing path normalization AT THE LOG BOUNDARY, not recontext-only — lock.py leaks whatever the caller passes, so a recontext-only fix cannot deliver 'all lock-event paths relative'. (4) Achievable measurable: after fix, lock.py acquire <ABS-path-inside-repo> logs a RELATIVE path -> grep -c '/Users/' on the newly-appended event = 0 (today ev226-229 leak); same for recontext add + notify init. PARTITION CHANGED: WB now also owns notify.py -> WA=harness_common.py+T-010.json+README.md; WB=lock.py+recontext.py+notify.py; WC=blackboard.py (still fully DISJOINT). Flagged option (b) shared hc.rel_path() helper would pull P-008 into harness_common.py (WA's file) = overlap; I recommend option (a) inline to keep the 3-way split. Also refreshed the two stale live counts (events 225->286, transcript 74->182) with a snapshot-at-T caveat (append-only, cited line numbers stable). REPLAY: grep -c '/Users/' events.jsonl == 9; confirm all 9 ts < 06:25:21Z; sed -n '199p;226p' for the notify+lock samples; grep -n path= .harness/bin/notify.py -> :200.
